coredata.dc:title: A new and facile nanosilver SPR colored method for ultratrace arsenic based on aptamer regulation of Au-doped carbon dot catalytic amplification
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Zhi-Hao
Lei, Kai-Ning
Li, Chong-Ning
Luo, Yang-He
Jiang, Zhi-Liang
coredata.dc:description: Here, Au-doped carbon dots (CDAu) nanosols with good stability were prepared by hydrothermal reaction method. We found that CDAu can efficiently catalyze the nanoreaction of reducing AgNO3 by glucose, and at 420 nm，the reaction products of yellow spherical silver nanosol exhibit an intense surface plasmon resonance (SPR) absorption peak. The nucleic acid aptamers (Apt) can be adsorbed on the surface of carbon dots, so that their catalytic activity was suppressed, the nanosilvers were reduced, the solution color becomes lighter, and the Abs value was weakened. When As3+ was added, it forms a stable conjugate with the Apt, releases free carbon dots, restored its catalytic activity, and the color and Abs signals enhanced linearly. Based on the Apt regulation and the catalytic amplification effect of CDAu on AgNO3-glucose, a new extremely sensitive SPR spectrophotometric method for the determination of arsenic ion content of 0.025–0.75 μg/L was established, and the detection limit of As3+ is 0.01 μg/L.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Arsenic
Aptamer
CDAu
Nanocatalysis
Nanosilver SPR
Spectrophotometry

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China
Key Technologies Research and Development Program

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Principles of Apt-mediated CDAu nanocatalysis-nanosilver SPR detection of As3+.
UV absorption spectrum of CDAu and CD sols. A: the a, b and c were 0.07; 0.14 and 0.29 mg/mL CDAu. B: the a, b and c were 0.07; 0.14 and 0.29 mg/mL CD.

SPR absorption spectra of the nanosilver sol systems. A CDAu-glucose-AgNO3 system, (a-g): 0.416 mM glucose +0.416 mM AgNO3 + (0; 0.0048; 0.0096; 0.0144; 0.0192; 0.0240 and 0.288) mg/mL CDAu. B CDAu-Apt-glucose-AgNO3 system, (a-g): 0.0144 mg/mL CDAu + 0.416 mM glucose+0.416 mM AgNO3 + (0; 0.00166; 0.0033; 0.005; 0.0067; 0.0083 and 0.01) μM Apt. C CDAu-Apt-As3+-glucose-AgNO3 system, (a-g): 0.005 μM Apt+0.0144 mg/mL CDAu + 0.416 mM glucose +0.416 mM AgNO3 + (0; 0.0945; 0.189; 0.2835; 0.378; 0.4725 and 0.567) μg/L As3+.
Fluorescence spectra of CDAu-Apt-As3+-glucose-AgNO3 system. (a-f): 0.005 μM Apt+0.192 mg/mL CDAu + 0.416 mM glucose +0.416 mM AgNO3+ (0; 0.189; 0.284; 0.378; 0.473 and 0.567) μg/L As3+.
Transmission electron microscope image. a CDAu. (b–d): 0.05 μmol/L Apt+0.0144 mg/mL CDAu + 0.416 mM glucose +0.416 mM AgNO3 + (0; 0.189 and 0.567) μg/L As3+.
Particle size distribution of different systems. a CDAu particle size distribution chart. (b–d): 0.05 μM Apt+0.0144 mg/mL CDAu + 0.416 mM glucose +0.416 mM AgNO3 + (0; 0.189 and 0.567) μg/L As3+.

Effect of experimental conditions. a CDAu + 0.0067 μM Apt +0.378 μg/L As3++0.67 mM glucose+0.5 mM AgNO3. b glucose+19.2 μg/mL CDAu + 0.0067 μM Apt +0.378 μg/LAs3+ +0.5 mM AgNO3. c AgNO3 + 19.2 μg/mL CDAu + 0.0067 mM Apt +0.378 μg/L As3+ +0.67 mM glucose. d Apt+19.2 μg/mL CDAu + 0.378 μg/L As3++0.67 Mm glucose+0.5 mM AgNO3. e 19.2 μg/mL CDAu + 0.0067 μM Apt +0.378 μg/LAs3++0.67 mM glucose +0.5 mM AgNO3. f 19.2 μg/mL CDAu + 0.0067 μM Apt +0.378 μg/L As3++0.67 mM glucose+0.5 mM AgNO3.
Working curve of the CDAu-Apt-As3+-glucose-AgNO3 system. 19.2 μg/mL CDAu + 0.0067 μM Apt + As3++0.67 mM glucose +0.5 mM AgNO3.
Comparison of some reported Apt molecular spectral methods for As3+.

row.entry: Target induced conformational change of complementary strand of aptamer and silica nanoparticles to cause the signal changes.
The Apt biosensor for As based on surfactant-induced gold nanoparticle aggregation.
As(III) induced aggregation of modified Au@Ag nanoparticles for surface enhanced Raman scattering detection.
The Apt reaction of AussDNA probe-As(III) caused RRS increasing at 278 nm due to nanogold aggregations.
Based on Apt regulation of Au-doped carbon dot catalytic colored reaction of AgNPs.
Highly sensitive, simple and low cost.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Sample measurement results of As3+ in water samples.

head.ce:title: A new and facile nanosilver SPR colored method for ultratrace arsenic based on aptamer regulation of Au-doped carbon dot catalytic amplification

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Food and Bioengineering, Hezhou University, Hezhou 542899, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Food and Bioengineering, Hezhou University, Hezhou 542899, China

Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Correspondence to: Y.-H. Luo, School of Food and Bioengineering, Hezhou University, Hezhou 542899, China.

Correspondence to: Z.-L. Jiang, Key Laboratory of Ecology of Rare and Endangered Species and Environmental Protection (Guangxi Normal University), Ministry of Education, Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology, Guilin 541004, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Guangxi Key Laboratory of Environmental Pollution Control Theory and Technology

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Here, Au-doped carbon dots (CDAu) nanosols with good stability were prepared by hydrothermal reaction method. We found that CDAu can efficiently catalyze the nanoreaction of reducing AgNO3 by glucose, and at 420 nm，the reaction products of yellow spherical silver nanosol exhibit an intense surface plasmon resonance (SPR) absorption peak. The nucleic acid aptamers (Apt) can be adsorbed on the surface of carbon dots, so that their catalytic activity was suppressed, the nanosilvers were reduced, the solution color becomes lighter, and the Abs value was weakened. When As3+ was added, it forms a stable conjugate with the Apt, releases free carbon dots, restored its catalytic activity, and the color and Abs signals enhanced linearly. Based on the Apt regulation and the catalytic amplification effect of CDAu on AgNO3-glucose, a new extremely sensitive SPR spectrophotometric method for the determination of arsenic ion content of 0.025–0.75 μg/L was established, and the detection limit of As3+ is 0.01 μg/L.

A sensitive SPR spectrophotometric method was established for arsenic ion, coupled the Apt reaction with the nanocatalytic reaction.
Unlabelled Image

ce:section.ce:para: Aptamer (Apt) was single-stranded oligonucleotides, usually composed of 20 to 100 bases, exponential enrichment system evolution of ligands was obtained from artificially constructed nucleic acid molecular libraries using in vitro screening [1]. The binding between Apt and target molecule was specific, and has more advantages and characteristics [2,3], such as the advantages of easy preservation, strong tissue permeability, strong specificity, simple and fast synthesis, wide applicability, non-toxicity, easy chemical modification and multifunctionality. Furthermore, the target molecules are abundant, in addition to proteins, small organic molecules, cells, viruses and metal ions [4–8]. The combination of specific Apt reaction with novel optical property of nanoparticles has been developed some molecular spectral methods such as spectrophotometry, fluorescence, Surface enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) and Resonance Rayleigh scattering (RRS). Jiang et al. [9] found a RRS method for the detection of Hg+ by Apt modified nanogold (AuNP) probes. The linear response of the decrease in RRS intensity (ΔI602nm) to Hg2+ concentration was between 0.1 and 400 nmol/L, and the detection limit is 0.03 nmol/L. Li et al. [10] established sensitive detection of aflatoxin B1 (AFB1) using 29-mer fluorescein (FAM) -labeled Apt, and the detection limit of AFB1 is 0.2 nmol/L. Li et al. [11] found a fast and simple method for protein colorimetric detection based on silver nanoparticle (AgNPs) catalyzed reduction of dye molecules. Respectively, the Apt-functionalized AgNPs and Apt-modified magnetic particles were used as detection probes and capture probes. It was found that the detection limit of thrombin was 0.2 nmol/L. To our best knowledge, the Apt regulation of carbon dots (CD) catalytic amplification AgNP colored reaction and its application to detection of As(III) were not reported.

CDs are a new type of nanoenzyme material, and have great application prospects in catalysis and chemical analysis [12]. The synthesis methods include laser etching method, arc discharge method, chemical oxidation method, microwave method and hydrothermal synthesis method [13–20]. Among them, the microwave method has the characteristics of simple and rapid preparation. Somayeh Mohammadi et al. [21] explored a FRET immunosensing technology for detecting CA15–3 tumor markers through an extremely biospecific interaction among the CA15–3 antigen and antibody functionalized luminescent CDs by Apt. The detection limit of CA15–3 tumor marker was 0.9 μU/mL. CDs are often used directly in chemically catalyzed reactions, but the synthesis and research of their composite nanoparticles has attracted widespread attention from chemists, especially with gold and silver nanoparticles doped composite nanomaterials containing metal ions. Not only can retain the characteristics and properties of the respective bodies of CDs and metal nanoparticles, but also bring new characteristics due to synergy. For example, Wang et al. [22] used graphene to reduce Au3+, and oxidized gold nanoparticles can be adsorbed on the surface of reduced grapheme oxide (RGO). The obtained gold nanoparticles/graphene composites were used to catalyze the oxidation of organic pollutants by nitrophenol. Aklima Nasrin et al. [23] synthesized a multi-color emitting CD with inherent ternary doping (N, S, P) by hydrothermal method. Subsequently, the combination of CD and folic acid (FA) made the human melanoma cancer cell SK-MEL28 targeted biological imaging and photosensitivity. Jayasmita et al. [24] synthesized CDs with fluorescence properties, and effectively detected nitrite ions in water through fluorescence quenching. The detection limit is 7.9 nmol/L and linear range is 2.3 μmol/L–7.7 mmol/L. Liu et al. [25] used low temperature solid state method to prepare quantum dots. A novel electrochemical luminescence (ECL) resonance energy transfer program was constructed for the detection limit is 0.01 f. DNA.

Surface plasmon resonance (SPR) is a sensitive surface analysis technology. It detects the change in dielectric constant caused by molecular adsorption on heavy metal films [26,27]. SPR nanotechnology has gained wide attention nowadays, and because of its unique advantages, such as fast in the complex matrix, highly specific to detect the target analyte, with high sensitivity, and has been widely applied in biosensors and chemical sensors. SERS is an important application of nanoplasmas. When the nanostructured surface plasma is irradiated with scattered light, the inelastic scattering of molecules adsorbed on its surface is enhanced. It is one of the few detection technologies that can be used at the molecular level [28]. However, its cost was high and reproducibility was not good. Spectrophotometry was very simple and low-cost instrumental analytical technique. It was combined with nanosol SPR effect to obtain high sensitivity easily. An AgNR SPR absorption method was fabricated for detection of 0.04–10 mg/L benzoyl peroxide, based on its oxidization of AgNR to decrease the peak at 480 nm [29]. Peng et al. [30] reported a colorimetric method for 0.5–35 μg/L Cu2+, based on AgNPs catalyzed by thiosulfate, the AgPRs SPR peak at 401 nm was reduced.

Arsenic is a common non-metallic element in nature. As3+ and As5+ are highly toxic. The human body can be poisoned by ingesting 1 mg of arsenic trioxide, so it is important for the determination of As3+ content in water. At present, the national water quality standard for the determining total arsenic in water quality is standard silver diethyldithiocarbamate spectrophotometric method, with the detection limit is 0.5 mg/L. This method is easy to operate, and has the advantages of economy, but the sensitivity is low. Mauana Schneider et al. [31] found a new method to determine arsenic in solid soil samples. A calibration curve was established using a standard solution, which linearly determined the arsenic content to 16 ng. The characteristic quality and detection limit were 22 pg As and 73 pg As, respectively. Orkun Alp et al. [32] described a fast non-chromatographic fluorescence spectrometry, which can be used to complete the determination of inorganic arsenic species at one time. The LOD values of As3+ is 27 pg/mL. Song et al. [33] proposed a sensitive method based on SERS and As3+-Apt to detect As3+. The successfully synthesized Au@Ag core nanoparticles not only had good dispersion, but also had high SERS efficiency. The concentration of As3+ ranged from 0.6 to 9 ng/mL, and the detection limit is 0.1 ng/mL. Li et al. [34] based on the fluorescence quenching effect of As5+ on rhodamine B, a resonance fluorescence quenching method was proposed for the detection of arsenic. Under the optimal conditions, the detection limit of arsenic is 1.30 × 10−9 g/mL. Up to date, the Apt-based CD-catalyzed silver nanoreaction-SPR spectrophotometry is rare in the determination of trace arsenic. Here, due to the high specificity of Apt and the excellent catalytic properties of Au-CDs, a new highly sensitive nanosilver SPR spectrophotometric method was established for the determination of As3+ content.

TM-0614P ceramic fiber muffle furnace (Beijing Yingan Meicheng Scientific Instrument Co., Ltd., Beijing, China), SYZ-550 quartz sub-boiling distilled water device (Jiangsu Crystal Glass Instrument Factory, Suzhou, China), HH-2 digital display constant temperature water bath pot (Guohua Electric Co., Ltd.), 16 K desktop centrifuge (Zhuhai Heima Medical Instrument Co., Ltd., Zhuhai, China), DHG-9023A electric constant temperature blast drying oven (Shanghai Jinghong Experimental Equipment Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China), SK3300B ultrasonic cleaner (Shanghai Kedao Ultrasonic Instrument Co., Ltd.), BSA224S electronic analytical balance (Beijing Sartorius Scientific Instrument Co., Ltd., Shanghai, China), TU-1901 dual-beam ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometer (Beijing General Analysis General Instrument Co., Ltd., Beijing, China), Hitachi F-7000 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Hitachi High-tech Corporation, Tokyo, Japan), and Talos F200S transmission electron microscope (Thermo Fisher Scientific China Co. Ltd., Shanghai, China) were used.

A 0.1% chloroauric acid (HAuCl4) was prepared: 1 g of chloroauric acid (the content of Au is not less than 47.8%) is added to water. After dissolving in the beaker, transfer it to a 25 mL volumetric flask and obtain a 3.33% chloroauric acid solution. For subsequent use, dilute to a 0.1% chloroauric acid solution and store it in the refrigerator for refrigerated storage. A 0.10 μM aptamer (Apt) with sequence of 5′-3′ATG CAA ACC CTT AAG AAA GTG GTC GTC CAA AAA ACC ATT G (Shanghai Shenggong Biological Co. Ltd., Shanghai, China) was diluted 1000 times. A 5.67 μg/L arsenic ion standard solution (As3+), 5.0 mM AgNO3(AR) and 5.0 mM glucose solution were prepared. All reagents used in the experiment are analytical pure. The water required in this experiment is secondary distilled water.

Using a ten-thousandth electronic balance, weigh 0.0883 g of trisodium citrate and 0.1082 g of urea, then use a graduated cylinder to measure 30 mL of sub-boiling distilled water with ultrasound to dissolve it in a beaker. Add 0.4 mL of 0.1% HAuCl4, continue to sonicate, then transfer to a polytetrafluoroethylene autoclave and tighten the seal. Then place it in a muffle furnace for hydrothermal reaction. The optimum temperature of the muffle furnace is set to 180 °C, and the reaction time is set to 2 h. After the reaction was completed, it is left to cool in ice water for 1 h. When the temperature of the autoclave reached room temperature, the main container was taken out to obtain a light yellow clear solution, that is, the Au carbon dot (CDAu) dope. Calculated based on the total carbon content, the C = 1.44 mg/mL CDAu solution was obtained. Before each subsequent use, it was shaken by ultrasound and diluted 5 times the original solution.

In a 5 mL graduated test tube, added 100 μL of 0.288 mg/mL CDAu, and 100 μL of 0.1 μM Apt, and a concentration gradient of 5.67 μg/mL of arsenic ion standard solution, shaken well, added 150 μL of 5 mM AgNO3 and 200 μL of 5 mM glucose solution, diluted to 1.5 mL with water, then the reaction was carried out in a 90 °C water bath for 50 min. After the completion, the tube was quickly taken out and cooled in cold water, and the Abs value was recorded.

Under optimal conditions, the reaction between glucose and AgNO3 proceeds very slowly, owing to the redox electron transfer being difficult. CDAu has rich surface electrons due to the coupled big π bond electrons with rich free electrons of Au atom. It enhanced the redox electron transfer, catalyzed the reduction of AgNO3 by glucose to produce yellow spherical nanosilver, and the SPR absorption peak was enhanced at 420 nm. When Apt exists, Apt will bind to CDAu and adsorb on its surface due to adsorption kinetics and intermolecular forces. The catalytic effect was weakened, the reaction rate was reduced, and the product concentration was generated slowly. After adding As3+, it specifically binds to the Apt. At this time, the CDAu is released and its catalytic activity is restored. When the concentration of As3+ raises, more CDAu desorbed on the Apt, the faster the reaction of reducing glucose to AgNO3, and the higher concentration of nanosilver produced, and the stronger absorption value. In a certain range, the value of Abs enhancement is linearly related to the concentration of arsenic ions added, and a new highly sensitive SPR spectrophotometric method for determining As3+ content was established (Fig. 1 ).

Absorption spectra of CDAu and CD were recorded (Fig.2 ), we can see that both have an absorption peak at 350 nm, the absorption vales of CDAu in the range of 400–500 nm were higher than the CD, owing to the coupled big π bond electrons with free electrons of Au atom. The absorption spectra showed that the glucose-AgNO3 reaction was slow and no absorption peak was observed. The CDAu-glucose-AgNO3 nanocatalytic system generated the yellow nanometer silver, which had an obvious characteristic SPR absorption peak at 420 nm, consistent with the results reported [21]. Within a certain range, as the concentration of CDAu raises, the catalytic effect on the system raises, and silver nanoparticles formed by the reaction increase, the color deepens, and the absorption intensity of system at 420 nm gradually increases (Fig. 3A). Adding a certain concentration of Apt to the CDAu-glucose-AgNO3 system, the Apt is wrapped on the surface of CDAu, inhibiting its catalytic ability. The reaction products of reducing AgNO3 by glucose are reduced, that is, nanosilver is reduced, and the color of the system solution becomes lighter. Within a certain range, as the increase of the Apt concentration, the absorption spectrum of the CDAu-Apt-glucose-AgNO3 system decreased at 420 nm by degrees (Fig. 3B). After adding a certain concentration of As3+ to the CDAu-Apt-glucose-AgNO3 system, the As3+ specifically bind to the Apt wrapped on the surface of the CDAu. At this time, CDAu was released and their catalytic activity was restored. As the dose of As3+ is increased, the more CDAu desorbed on the Apt, the faster the reaction of reducing glucose to AgNO3, and the more nanosilver produced, the deeper the solution color and the absorption intensity. And the SPR peak at 420 nm was selected foe determination of As(III) (Fig. 3C). We also examined the CD catalysis, it was lower than the CDAu, due to CD being of less surface electrons.

CDs are fluorescent, so we studied the fluorescence spectra of CDAu. When the fixed emission wavelength (λem) of the fluorescence spectrometer is set to 450 nm, the CDAu generates an excitation peak at 366 nm, so 366 nm is selected as the excitation wavelength (λex), with slit of 5 nm and the voltage of 350 V. For the CDAu-Apt-As3+-glucose-AgNO3 system, there is a fluorescence peak at 440 nm (Fig. 4 ). With the increase of As3+ concentration, the fluorescence peak intensity is increased by degrees, as shown in Fig. 4. This is because the Apt in the system encapsulates the CDAu, and the fluorescence intensity decreases. When As3+ was added at an appropriate concentration, arsenic ions specifically bound to the Apt. At this time, CDAu was released, the fluorescence characteristics were restored, and the intensity of the fluorescence emission peaks gradually increasing, and a fluorescence spectrophotometric method could be set up for the detection of As3+, but it is not as convenient as spectrophotometric method, and the fluorescence changing value was not big.

After diluting the carbon dots prepared in the experiment by a certain multiple, a 3 μL solution was dropped evenly on the copper mesh, then scanned the sample with TEM (Fig. 5 ). As shown by TEM (Fig. 5a), CDAu tends to have a spherical structure and the average particle size is about 4 nm. The TEMs of Apt-As3+-CDAu-glucose-AgNO3 reaction systems (where As3+ concentration is 0, 0.189, 0.567 μg/L, respectively) was obtained. According to Fig. 5b, c and d, the blank system without As3+, only has a small amount of particles with a size of 50 nm. When As3+ is present in the system, the carbon dots wrapped by the Apt are released, and a large number of particles are formed and aggregated, the average particle sizes is 30 and 40 nm respectively.

According to Fig. 6 , the average particle diameter of CDAu is 80 nm, due to being of aggregations. When no As3+ exists in the system, the particle size of Apt-CDAu-glucose-AgNO3 reaction system is 90 nm. When a certain amount of As3+ was added, a particle size of the system can reach 200 nm, and the size gradually becomes uniform.

The experimental conditions were examined for the Apt-As3+-CDAu-glucose-AgNO3 system. First, optimized the concentration of Au carbon dot (CDAu) in the system, added 0.378 μg/L As3+ solution and different concentrations of CDAu, and the rest follow the experimental steps. It can be seen from Fig. 7a that when 19.2 μg/mL CDAu is added, the system ΔA420nm is the strongest, so the CDAu concentration is selected to be 19.2 μg/mL. The glucose concentration was considered. As shown in Fig. 7b, when 0.67 mmol/L glucose is added, the system ΔA420nm is the strongest, so the glucose concentration is 0.67 mmol/L. The concentration of AgNO3 was optimized, according to Fig. 7c, when 0.5 mmol/L AgNO3 was added, the system ΔA420nm was the strongest, so the AgNO3 concentration was selected to be 0.5 mmol/L. As shown in Fig. 7d, when 0.0067 μmol/L Apt is added, the system ΔA420nm is the strongest, so the Apt concentration is 0.0067 μmol/L. As shown in Fig. 7e and f, when the water bath temperature is 90 °C for 50 min, the system ΔA420nm is the strongest, so 90 °C for 50 min is used.

According to the experimental procedure, the interference of coexisting interfering ions on determination of 0.567 μg/L As3+ in the experimental system was studied. When the relative error is not more than 10%, results Table 2 show that 100 times Ba2+, I+, Zn2+, Co2+, Mn2+, Cr3+, Cr6+, NO3 −, Al3+, Mg2+, NH4 + and IO3−, 50 times of SO4 2−, Br− and Cu2+, 20 times of NO2 −, Fe2+ and Hg2+, 10 times SO3 2− does not disturb with the measurement. This method has good selectivity for detecting As3+.

Water samples were taken from three surrounding sewage discharge locations at least 3 km apart, and the sewage samples were tested according to experimental procedure to demonstrate the feasibility of this method. Treatment of sewage samples was as follow, used a vacuum suction pump to filter three water samples to remove impurities such as sediments, suspended matter, etc. Next, a microporous membrane with a pore diameter of 0.45 μm was used to perform secondary filtration on the sample. The As3+ standardization and recovery experiments were examined for the water samples. The relative standard deviation (RSD) is 5.23%–9.50%, and the recovery rate was 93.1%–105.3% (Table 3 ).

In summary, we found that the as-prepared Au-doped carbon dots (CDAu) exhibit strong catalysis of glucose reduction of AgNO3 to form SPR effect of nanosilver. Combining specific Apt reaction with the nanocatalytic amplification signal, a novel and simple SPR absorption method was developed for detection of trace As3+ in water, the results are satisfactory.

Zhi-Hao Zhang: Resources, Writing - original draft, Methodology. Kai-Ning Lei: Resources, Writing - original draft. Chong-Ning Li: Resources, Writing - original draft, Methodology. Yang-He Luo: Supervision, Conceptualization, Methodology.Zhi-Liang Jiang: Supervision, Conceptualization, Methodology.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The manuscript entitled “A new and facile nanosilver SPR colored method for ultratrace arsenic based on aptamer regulation of Au-doped carbon dot catalytic amplification” has no conflicts to declare.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was funded by the National Key Research and Development Program of China [grant number 2018YFD0901003], and National Natural Science Foundation of China [grant numbers 21767004, 21667006].

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Gold nanoparticle-based colorimetric assay for the direct detection of cancerous cells

sb:title.sb:maintitle: I-Tryptophan-capped carbon quantum dots for the sensitive and selective fluorescence detection of mercury ion in aqueous solution

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Isolation and characterization of fluorescent nanoparticles from pristine and oxidized electric arc-produced single-walled carbon nanotubes
